docs.title: Stallion Fluoro Chemicals
docs.text: Stallion Fluoro Chemicals Operated counter; don't buy fresh at this price He called it an operated counter with more noise than performance, hard to comment on. It disappointed badly versus its segment. If it is only a small rally, exit; no one should buy fresh at this price. After the rights issue is done, look at the price and quarterly performance before deciding.

docs.title: Hitech Pipes
docs.text: Hitech Pipes Avoid; exit on rallies Not a great stock; it moves in a 30-40 range on and off every 3-4 months, but has fallen continuously since the last quarter's result, going below 75 and bottoming near 87-90. Top line rose but bottom line did not, and raw-material/commodity costs are likely to rise. He would avoid it and exit on rallies.

docs.title: Sezzle India
docs.text: Sezzle India Accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns. Caller asked about entering at the current price point. Kutumba Rao said buy/accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns. By long term he means hold a minimum of more than one year (don't ask back in three or six months).

docs.title: PFC
docs.text: PFC PFC.NS Financial Services Good dividend-yield bet as an FD alternative, alongside NTPC. In the same FD-alternative / dividend-yield discussion he said PFC too would be a good bet. Check the dividend record date and hold with a 3-5 year horizon.

docs.title: PNB
docs.text: PNB PNBGILTS.NS Financial Services Large dividend payer; add on a pullback for dividend yield. Mentioned alongside Canara Bank as a large dividend payer. With PSU banks slightly high now, attempt on a fall for dividend yield above the FD level.

docs.title: WPIL (Worthington Pumps)
docs.text: WPIL (Worthington Pumps) Good niche engineering company; slow and steady, can go back up to 450-500. Hold. Caller holds 5,000 shares around 550. A good company with good market share on the engineering side, pumps for a niche segment. No immediate jump expected, but slow and steady it can go back up to 450-500 easily, so hold, no worry. When buying such large quantity, buy in tranches.

docs.title: Newjaisa (News & Soft)
docs.text: Newjaisa (News & Soft) Don't chase; sustains only with follow-up buying. Mandatory stop-loss above 600. Caller bought around 600 in a hurry. Buying it repeatedly is unnecessary; it made a large move (crossed 600, up to ~620) but only sustains with follow-up buying, and fell sharply after results (bottomed ~475 over three months). If buying above 600, definitely keep a trading stop-loss; entering at higher levels is risky. The Thursday spike likely came on an unconfirmed social-media trigger.

docs.title: GRSE (Garden Reach Shipbuilders)
docs.text: GRSE (Garden Reach Shipbuilders) Hold; becoming attractive at current levels Results are all good. Like other PSU shipyards many bought the 2024 peak and got stuck; touching old highs will take time. Becoming attractive to buy around 2,400-2,500. Don't sell, hold.

docs.title: RateGain Travel
docs.text: RateGain Travel RATEGAIN.NS Technology Hold cautiously; watch margins/acquisition Dec-quarter top line jumped but margins are disappointing; cash-rich and many await an acquisition that could take it back to 700-800 levels, but if cash exhausts without one there is downside risk. They paid Rs 12 cr interest (took a loan) which must be watched. Hold; watch one more quarter; SaaS model faces possible AI disruption.

docs.title: PFC (Power Finance Corporation)
docs.text: PFC (Power Finance Corporation) Accumulate on every decline Buy without any doubt; accumulate on every decline. Post the REC merger this becomes one very big entity. Definitely a good buy at current levels.

docs.title: BlueStone Jewellery
docs.text: BlueStone Jewellery Avoid; exit on rallies IPO-euphoria encashment; a ~Rs 7,000-8,000 cr market cap is not justified for a fashion-jewellery company of this size. Already at its 52-week low and could halve from here. It is now a large-player game (Aditya Birla's Indriya). Exit on rallies.

docs.title: Raymond Lifestyle
docs.text: Raymond Lifestyle RAYMONDLSL.NS Consumer Cyclical Hold; exit on rallies Good branding but flat and not growing, turnover stagnant for long; same risk as BlueStone applies. Watch whether the company restructures and kick-starts the business with new brands; otherwise it is a good takeover target. Exit on rallies.

docs.title: Sammaan Capital
docs.text: Sammaan Capital SAMMAANCAP.NS Financial Services Speculative bet only An extremely speculative counter, frequently in the ban list; swings to 175-180 then easily back to 135-140. Treat only as a speculative bet. At ~145-148 it can prepare a short-term rally and may attempt 160-180 again.

docs.title: Kaynes Technology
docs.text: Kaynes Technology KAYNES.NS Technology Wait one more quarter for fresh buying Corrected to ~3,300-3,400 after a brokerage report, recovered and now sustains at ~4,000; last quarter was flat. Would wait for another quarter of performance before fresh buying.

docs.title: Quality Walls
docs.text: Quality Walls Buy nearer 22-23, in March HUL-demerged ice-cream entity, double down-circuit; market questions the ~Rs 6,000 cr market cap for an ice-cream company. Buyable closer to 22-23 levels, in March, with the summer season approaching.

docs.title: Anup Engineering
docs.text: Anup Engineering ANUP.NS Industrials Hold; low-liquidity caution A good company; hype has gone and it is now at a realistic valuation (~1,800-1,900 vs a prior 3,500). Results flat but competent, with an encouraging recent earnings-call transcript. Hold; watch carefully due to low volumes/liquidity which can cause sharp moves.

docs.title: Talbros Automotive
docs.text: Talbros Automotive TALBROAUTO.NS Consumer Cyclical One of the better auto-ancillary names, decent results Kutumba Rao said hold Talbros Auto, calling it one of the better auto-ancillary companies with decent results. It is near its 52-week high (recently ~300, stable ~280) with signs of crossing the 52-week highs again, helped by auto ancillaries doing well lately.

docs.title: Senco Gold
docs.text: Senco Gold SENCO.NS Consumer Cyclical Eye-popping Dec result + precious-metals rally He said hold Senco Gold. The December result was eye-popping: revenue ~Rs 3,000 crore (up 100% QoQ) and net profit from Rs 53 crore to Rs 267 crore. With 320-330 as a major base, the stock can attempt 400 again, helped by speculator fancy and the precious-metals rally.

docs.title: TVS Supply Chain Solutions
docs.text: TVS Supply Chain Solutions TVSSCS.NS Industrials Turnaround result, new high likely shortly He said buy TVS Supply Chain on dips. After good turnaround results the stock already rose ~25-30% from around 95-96 and a new high looks likely very shortly. Since the recent rally was sharp, he would buy on a small correction toward 107-108 over the next 1-2 months.

docs.title: Sharath Industries
docs.text: Sharath Industries Former dark horse; controlled/operated stock He said if the holder wants to hold Sharath Industries, hold it. A former 'dark horse' (flagged at 40) on the aqua side that gave good returns earlier; recently dull with low volumes, around 170, close to its 52-week high. He cautioned it is more of a controlled/operated stock.

docs.title: Tilaknagar Industries
docs.text: Tilaknagar Industries Poor recent results, promoter pledging; wait and see He advised waiting and watching rather than buying Tilaknagar Industries now. The recent results were poor (a loss despite a dramatic top-line jump and high capex), and promoters pledged maximum shares. He would wait to see whether a turnaround comes in the coming quarter before buying.

docs.title: Divi's Laboratories
docs.text: Divi's Laboratories DIVISLAB.NS Healthcare Bought near 52-week low; large pharma name He said hold Divi's Labs with nothing to fear. The caller bought near the 52-week low (~5,000; 52wk low 4,941) and the stock is already at ~6,100 (once went to 7,000). It is one of the better stocks to hold in an uncertain atmosphere, being one of the largest pharma market caps at Rs 1.6 lakh crore.

docs.title: News & Soft (Nucleus Software / News & Soft)
docs.text: News & Soft (Nucleus Software / News & Soft) Fell with IT; nothing wrong company-specific On the caller's 50%-loss position, Kutumba Rao said it fell along with all IT stocks and there is nothing specifically wrong at the company. Recovery is likely only after IT sentiment improves (Ramakrishna added the chart is very weak with weak recent results).

docs.title: Hindustan Aeronautics
docs.text: Hindustan Aeronautics HAL.NS Industrials Monopoly PSU; correction is a buying opportunity He said hold HAL, citing its monopoly status as a Government of India PSU counter. The current corrective mode is a buying opportunity, not a selling opportunity.

docs.title: Kaynes Technologies
docs.text: Kaynes Technologies Exit on rallies; relief rallies not holding Recently controversial with news flow; even after clarifications the relief rallies are not holding up. Advised exiting on rallies. (Caller was in a 20% loss.)

docs.title: Marksons Pharma
docs.text: Marksons Pharma Exit on rallies; weak December result December result was weak, below expectations, with a sharply lower bottom line. Advised exiting on rallies, even if it comes close up to 200.

docs.title: Amara Raja Energy
docs.text: Amara Raja Energy ARE&M.NS Industrials Hold; transformative capex stage, fundamentally strong Company is in a transformative stage with heavy capex on new-generation products and lithium batteries; the Telangana unit is yet to be commissioned. Stock may stay weak near term with weak results, but fundamentally extremely strong, so hold; no need to sell at these levels.

docs.title: Satvik Green Energy
docs.text: Satvik Green Energy Hold; recently listed, needs time Recently listed and needs more time frame. As of now, hold.

docs.title: HPL Electric
docs.text: HPL Electric HPL.NS Industrials Hold; recently listed, needs time Grouped with the recently listed names; needs more time frame to play out. Hold for now.

docs.title: Reliance Infrastructure
docs.text: Reliance Infrastructure Dark-horse holding/implementation company; news rallies fizzle Should be seen as a holding/implementation company. Anil Ambani group on-and-off news flow brings brief rallies that fizzle out immediately. Not a counter to pay much attention to; called it a dark-horse counter.

docs.title: Jammu & Kashmir Bank
docs.text: Jammu & Kashmir Bank Partial exit; best of its time likely over Results are good, but he thinks the best of its time is over and it has reached a peak. Advised a partial exit at the 115-117 levels. Business may improve after peace/stability, but outperforming returns are not expected.

docs.title: Bell Rice Industries
docs.text: Bell Rice Industries Buy only on declines; if held, trailing stop and hold Shows continuous height and action and has gained some fancy, but he treats it as a highly speculative bet (flat December result yet jumped from 160-165 to 190). Buy only on declines; if already bought, put a trailing stop and hold.

docs.title: Usha Martin
docs.text: Usha Martin UMESLTD.NS Consumer Defensive Wait; buy near major support 380-390 on weekly weakness At current levels (around 420-430) do not buy; wait. Recommended at 60-70 rupees three years back, it touched 500. Major support is around 380-390. Buy when that level comes during weekly weakness. No fresh buying; book some profits At these levels, no fresh buying. Advised booking some profits since lower levels would have been available.

docs.title: Poly Medicure
docs.text: Poly Medicure POLYMED.NS Healthcare Hold; flagship med-equipment company A flagship medical-equipment company (not small) that performs well. Corrected to near its 52-week low on subdued recent results, but not a price to sell. Maybe exit on rallies once it reaches the 1,580-1,600 levels. (Caller was in ~50% loss.)

docs.title: REC
docs.text: REC Hold; don't sell, P/E ~5 and high dividend May stay weak until the PFC merger ratio gets clarity and Government of India approval comes. Don't sell, though, because it trades at a P/E of about 5 (literally peanuts) and is a high-dividend payer. Take any decision only after total restructuring clarity. (Caller in ~50% loss.)

docs.title: Vikran Engineering
docs.text: Vikran Engineering VIKRAN.NS Industrials No buy now; enter ~75-76 after a further ~15% fall At current levels, don't buy. Already at its 52-week low with no great result, but maybe some value as a manufacturing company. Advised entering close to around the 75-76 levels, i.e. after roughly another 15% correction.

docs.title: JSW Energy
docs.text: JSW Energy JSWENERGY.NS Utilities Add and hold; one of the better energy plays For a caller sitting on a 15-16% loss, he said you can both add and hold; no need to be afraid. Called it one of the better stars in the energy space with a strong acquisition portfolio that will grow well.

docs.title: Narayana Hrudayalaya
docs.text: Narayana Hrudayalaya NH.NS Healthcare Buy after results (13th) Recently attempted 52-week highs and is modestly consolidating. Not a cheap stock at about 80 PE, but he expects a good result (tomorrow, the 13th) and advised buying after the result.

docs.title: HFCL
docs.text: HFCL HFCL.NS Technology Hold; transforming into a defense company Good results; post-results it is consolidating strongly above 70. It is slowly transforming from an optical-fiber company into a defense company, and he wouldn't be surprised if it attempts 100 again. Hold.

docs.title: Syngene International
docs.text: Syngene International Exit on rallies; very weak Near a 52-week low with weak December-quarter results; he advised against averaging the caller's 50% loss. Financial performance is not promising at all, so exit on rallies.

docs.title: Quess Corp
docs.text: Quess Corp QUESS.NS Industrials High debt is working capital, no concern On a viewer's debt worry, he explained the debt is mostly working capital, normal for services firms. Borrowings of 175 crores against trade receivables of 185 crores, with neutral net cash flows; income growing and margins improving. Nothing to worry about, just invest and hold.

docs.title: CCL Products
docs.text: CCL Products CCL.NS Consumer Defensive Accumulate on a modest dip Strong result; the stock hit 1,070, a 52-week high. He expects a modest correction toward 930-940 and said to accumulate at that level. Existing holders can hold.

docs.title: Canfin Homes
docs.text: Canfin Homes Stay in Canfin, don't shift to RBL/AU Canfin is still strong with good post-result performance, so he would not suggest switching out of it (to RBL Bank or AU Small Finance Bank). Noted that AU, converting from small-finance to a regular bank and consolidating near 1,000, is momentum-wise currently the stronger name.

docs.title: DB Realty (Valor Estate)
docs.text: DB Realty (Valor Estate) Speculative only; prefer frontline realty Said there are much better companies; treat it as speculative only or for those tracking inside info. Name changed to Valor Estate; came below 100 and mildly recovered on December results. Prefer frontline realty names like Brigade or Prestige.

docs.title: Nifty 26000 monthly call option
docs.text: Nifty 26000 monthly call option Hold but trim above 25,900 Held at 180; he suggested it yesterday too. As of now hold, but reduce the position when the market comes back above 25,900, because indications of crossing 26,000 are not appearing this series, which feels tight.

docs.title: ONGC
docs.text: ONGC ONGC.NS Energy Hold; dividend stock near 52-week high Definitely hold; the holder (bought at 294, now ~270) is not in a big loss since the stock is near its 52-week high. Avail the second interim dividend (record date 12th). The stock will likely attempt 310-320 once, where the position can be trimmed. It is more of a dividend-paying stock with long-run capital appreciation too.

docs.title: Maruti Suzuki
docs.text: Maruti Suzuki MARUTI.NS Consumer Cyclical Accumulate slowly on dips toward 15,000 Post-results it went to 17,000-plus, corrected, and is now around 15,200. Accumulate slowly from here; strong support has developed at 14,600-700, so buy whenever it comes closer to 15,000. As the country's largest carmaker it sustains profitability and margins and improved quarter-on-quarter.

docs.title: Healthcare Global Enterprises
docs.text: Healthcare Global Enterprises HCG.NS Healthcare Long-term buy only; better to enter after ex-rights If you have a long-term view, definitely buy, but no spectacular short-term gains. The stock corrected from ~800 to ~600 and the result was disappointing (slipped into a loss). With a proposed rights issue it will trade cum-rights, so it is better to think and take it after ex-rights.

docs.title: Electronics Mart India
docs.text: Electronics Mart India Avoid; weak bottom line, only a retailer No positivity here. It is more of a retailer (not a manufacturer), so margins won't improve much, especially as e-commerce is taking sales even in fast-moving electrical goods. Profit came modestly (~Rs 29 cr) despite some top-line growth. If anything, exit even on a rally toward ~189.

docs.title: PTC Engineering
docs.text: PTC Engineering Avoid for now; lower-top lower-bottom pattern For now avoid; it should be bought only on declines. The stock has formed lower-top, lower-bottom continuously for the last two quarters, so it won't jump easily. Maybe it reclaims 1,000 again; buy only if it crosses 1,000.

docs.title: RBL Bank
docs.text: RBL Bank Hold but exit on bounce; narrow band 300-340, no immediate move to old 400+ highs. The worst is likely over but the stock moves in a narrow band between 300 and 340. The viewer bought at 340; that price will come easily and when it does, he says exit. He does not expect the old highs of 400-plus to come immediately.

docs.title: Nifty ETF
docs.text: Nifty ETF IVZINNIFTY.NS Safest route if you can't bear individual-stock volatility (vs large-caps). Asked about replacing individual large-cap stocks with a Nifty ETF, he says ETF is a safe play. If you are in individual stocks and cannot bear their ups, downs and volatility, a Nifty ETF is the safest route - no doubt about it.

docs.title: Lokesh Machines
docs.text: Lokesh Machines LOKESHMACH.NS Industrials Hold, don't shift to Avantel yet; check result, may go 200+ after March then shift. Holder of 200 free shares asked about switching to Avantel. He says check the result first - Lokesh was hit indirectly by American tariffs and some sanctions, seen in the stock. The best of times are over and it corrected to 200, but after March there are signs of it going to 200-plus; shift at that time, not now.

docs.title: PPL Pharma
docs.text: PPL Pharma Switch from PPL Pharma to Laurus Labs - no doubt. The viewer is at about a 15% loss in PPL Pharma and asked about switching to Laurus. He says from PPL to Laurus you can shift, no doubt about it.

docs.title: Sequent Scientific
docs.text: Sequent Scientific Hold; first post-name-change quarterly given optimistically; good long-term returns. Hold Sequent Scientific. After the name change, the first conference was just the other day - this is the first-quarter result, given optimistically. The earnings-call transcript has to be seen, but in the long term it can give good returns.

docs.title: Nippon India Small Cap Fund
docs.text: Nippon India Small Cap Fund Continue SIP; performing nicely. On Rekha's mutual-fund SIPs (Nippon India Small Cap, Kotak Flexi Cap, HDFC Large & Mid, Mirae Asset Large & Mid), he says they are fine - all performing nicely. Continue the SIPs as they give good returns in the long run.

docs.title: Kotak Flexi Cap Fund
docs.text: Kotak Flexi Cap Fund Continue SIP; performing nicely. Part of Rekha's SIP set that he endorses continuing - all are performing nicely and give good long-run returns.

docs.title: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund
docs.text: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms ICICI Pru/Motilal Oswal Large&Mid but good long-run. He says HDFC Large & Mid (and Mirae Asset) sometimes underperform compared to ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise in the long run they give good returns; continue the SIP.

docs.title: Mirae Asset Large and Mid Cap Fund
docs.text: Mirae Asset Large and Mid Cap Fund Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms peers but good long-run returns. Part of Rekha's SIP set; sometimes underperforms ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise gives good long-run returns - continue.

docs.title: IRB InvIT
docs.text: IRB InvIT IRBINVIT-IV.NS Buy; ~13% dividend, parent toll revenue up 6500->7500 cr YoY; five toll assets + a HAM asset. Definitely buy IRB InvIT - no need to fear. The parent IRB Infra's toll revenues are strong and factorisation benefit accrues to this trust; toll revenue jumped year-on-year from 6,500 crore to 7,500 crore. It shows ~13% dividend, is near its 52-week high, and has five toll assets in Western and Southern India plus a HAM asset - looks stronger.

docs.title: Maithan Alloys
docs.text: Maithan Alloys MAITHANALL.NS Basic Materials Hold if you have the time horizon; undervalued, deploys cash into equities; else switch to MF. Maithan Alloys is an undervalued company that deploys its cash into listed/unlisted equities rather than core business (it bought ~40,000-50,000 NSE shares at ~1,000, now doubled). If you have the time horizon, invest/hold; otherwise there are much better companies and a mutual fund is the better route.

docs.title: DCX Systems
docs.text: DCX Systems DCXINDIA.NS Industrials Hold and wait for the 12th board-meeting (Dec) result; turnaround would help, else may retest 52-wk low. For a viewer at 352 now at a 50% loss, he says it disappointed since its hyped listing - even the September result was disappointing. There is a board meeting on the 12th for the December-quarter result; if a turnaround shows, the stock improves, otherwise it may retest the 52-week low. Having waited this long, it is better to hold and see the result.

docs.title: Aditya Birla Capital
docs.text: Aditya Birla Capital ABCAPITAL.NS Financial Services Hold, don't book profit; group flagship in capital markets, near 52-week high - keep a trailing stop and let it run. For a viewer at average 120, he says hold and don't book profit. It is the group's flagship company in the capital markets and the stock is near its 52-week high. In stocks touching 52-week highs you should keep a trading stop, protect profits and let it run rather than selling just because it has risen.

docs.title: Rolex Rings
docs.text: Rolex Rings ROLEXRINGS.NS Industrials Buy near 110; auto-ancillary, stock split done, liquidity up Rolex Rings is fine; it did a stock split and liquidity increased. Buy it only when it comes closer to 110. It is an auto-ancillary company, not comparable to broking names like Groww.

docs.title: Prime Focus
docs.text: Prime Focus Avoid at current levels after 200% 6-month run The stock rose 200% in just six months (about 80 to 280) on speculation and is at 52-week highs. There are doubts it can rise further from these levels unless the company delivers another successful blockbuster VFX project. As of now do not buy; consider only on a strong decline.

docs.title: Advanced Agrolife
docs.text: Advanced Agrolife Wait another quarter; weak Dec result, micro-cap Recently listed with only one quarter of performance, and the December result was not good at all, leaving the stock near its 52-week low around 130. Fundamentals do not seem to justify the valuation. It is a micro-cap (about Rs 850 crore market cap). Wait for another quarter.

docs.title: SBFC Finance
docs.text: SBFC Finance SBFC.NS Financial Services Buy for short/short-medium term only, not long term The result has come and is good, but the stock is not a fancy one. Keeping that in view, it can be bought for the short or short-medium term. Long-term bets in such companies do not work well.

docs.title: Indegene
docs.text: Indegene INDGN.NS Healthcare Hold; weak-looking but high potential, not cheap Hold; no need to fear. Performance may feel weak but potential is high, and such future-discounting stocks tend to move. It is not a cheap stock, around Rs 12,000 crore market cap. The December profit dip was a one-off, so no need to exit even if the stock is weak now.

docs.title: Tata Capital
docs.text: Tata Capital TATACAP.NS Financial Services Buy/accumulate; medium-term target 425-430 Hold and buy. Earlier advised to accumulate even at 320-330, with a medium-term target of 425-430. Definitely a buy.

docs.title: Eternal
docs.text: Eternal Avoid as investment; now a trading/F&O bet It has become an institutional stock since going into F&O, with swift 5-10% weekly moves on and off. It has become more of a trading bet than an investment bet as of now.

docs.title: Transformers and Rectifiers India (TARIL)
docs.text: Transformers and Rectifiers India (TARIL) Even good Dec results couldn't lift the stock; exit on rallies The stock was at 520 in Sep-Oct and has halved; even after good December top-line and bottom-line results it couldn't rise, so 'we are missing out on something.' Avoid; if already held, exit on rallies around 310-320 and shift to a better-managed energy/transformer company like Hitachi Energy.

docs.title: Hitachi Energy
docs.text: Hitachi Energy POWERINDIA.NS Industrials Record results (+90% net profit); buy on every dip Net profit rose 90% YoY with highest-ever revenue, EBITDA (180cr to 370cr) and PAT/PBT. He had recommended buying it on every dip; the stock opened strongly and is in upper circuit. He also flags it as the preferred switch destination from Transformers & Rectifiers and Kaynes.

docs.title: Standard Glass Lining / Standard Engineering
docs.text: Standard Glass Lining / Standard Engineering Range-bound 120-140 absent game-changing news Range-bound 120-140 unless game-changing news comes. Glass showed ~23% growth and consolidated Q3 ~37% YoY, but PAT only rose ~26-27%. The bio-process-systems entry will take more time to outperform. 140 was the issue price; no need to sell or add - hold.

docs.title: Bharti Hexacom
docs.text: Bharti Hexacom BHARTIHEXA.NS Communication Services One of the 3-yr outperformers; exit only on rallies if needed Over the last three years it has surprisingly been one of the outperforming stocks and only came down a bit in mild corrections. A decent stock to hold; if one wants to exit, do so on rallies, but at this time hold. A good Bharti Group company.

docs.title: Windlas Biotech
docs.text: Windlas Biotech WINDLAS.NS Healthcare Buy at attractive valuation; stock-picker's paradise now Asked by a viewer (Saira Sriram), he said to definitely lock it down and buy, as the stock now has an attractive valuation. He added that with the budget over and only the Indo-US deal ahead, it is now a stock-picker's paradise - favour companies that benefit indirectly from the budget or have completed expansion capex.

docs.title: ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 Fund / Bandhan Infrastructure Fund
docs.text: ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 Fund / Bandhan Infrastructure Fund Switch out of broader-market and infra themes into diversified funds A viewer (Nagarajan) was at a loss in ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 (broader-market) and Bandhan Infrastructure (sectoral). Kutumba Rao said negative sentiment is heavier in the broader market versus Nifty 50, and infra is unlikely to see any big move even post-budget. He advised exiting these as far as possible and moving into diversified flexi-cap or multi-cap funds, and warned against chasing gold/silver - asset allocation must be followed strictly.

docs.title: IREDA
docs.text: IREDA IREDA.NS Financial Services Hold; IPO froth cooled, watch NPA recovery A viewer is at a ~29% loss. He called it a decent company whose speculative IPO-time activity has cooled off. A recent rise in NPAs is a concern and the recovery needs watching, so he advised holding for now.

docs.title: Morepen Labs
docs.text: Morepen Labs Hold; demerger of medical-devices business could add momentum A viewer is at a ~42% loss. Given the big correction in mid- and small-cap stocks, he advised holding. Their medical-devices business is heading for a listing/demerger, and after that news there is a chance of some momentum in the stock.

docs.title: Somany Ceramics
docs.text: Somany Ceramics SOMANYCERA.NS Industrials Flat results; near 52-week low, hold for post-Budget infra push Somany's results were flat - top line and bottom line both flat with no big improvement, so no need to average now. Maybe hold for the Budget; if there is an infra push, building/construction/infra companies are likely to rise post-Budget. It is closer to the 52-week low, so it's not the time to sell.

docs.title: Nippon Gold BeES
docs.text: Nippon Gold BeES Don't take a gold loan to invest; use savings only A viewer wanted to pledge his gold for a Rs 16 lakh loan to invest in Gold BeES. Kutumba Rao said no - never invest with borrowed money, use only savings. If gold corrects (possible within six months to a year) the leveraged position is risky; understand that risk before acting.

docs.title: HG Infra
docs.text: HG Infra Hold all infra ahead of Budget; Dec results awaited He said to compulsorily hold all infra stocks ahead of the Budget, as it is a sector the government must address. Fundamentally a good counter, no doubt. September results were soft due to monsoon (lower execution); December results are still awaited but likely better with no monsoon. If asked, hold.

docs.title: Gold ETF (Tata Gold ETF / Nippon Gold BeES / ICICI Pru Gold)
docs.text: Gold ETF (Tata Gold ETF / Nippon Gold BeES / ICICI Pru Gold) Continue gold SIP/ETF; weak dollar and central-bank buying support gold On a viewer's gold-vs-Jio-Finance SIP question, Kutumba Rao framed them as different asset classes and advised allocating across both. He endorsed continuing or adding gold via ETF (Tata Gold, Nippon Gold BeES or ICICI Pru Gold), noting these gave roughly 95-100% returns over the last year. He argued a weakening dollar index and central banks shifting reserves from treasuries into gold support gold over the long term.

docs.title: 2026-07-02 Summary — Business Breakfast, 2 July 2026 (TV5 Money)
docs.text: Summary — Business Breakfast, 2 July 2026 (TV5 Money) Global backdrop Global cues mixed/slightly negative but GIFT Nifty positive , indicating a 90 100 point gap up open. docs.title: 2026-05-13 Summary — Business Breakfast, 13 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
docs.text: Summary — Business Breakfast, 13 May 2026 (TV5 Money) Global backdrop US markets closed lower last night; recovered somewhat from the lows. Gift Nifty signaled a slightly positive open. Core driver of the turmoil: crude oil disruption at the Strait of Hormuz . Travel through it is shifting from "free" to "paid," pushing logistics/transport costs up. Kutumba Rao thinks 80–90 dollars/barrel could be the "new normal" for crude even if the war stops (old Rs 50–60 won't reset). LNG cost flagged as the next danger — roughly 70 dollars per MWh ; a sharp rise would hit LNG using industries. Trump visiting China today — watched as a potential catalyst; any trade deal / tariff announcement could give global markets some relief. "AI fear" (intangible) plus tangible asset monopolies (crude) seen as a double squeeze on countries like India with little presence in either. Indian markets Yesterday: Rs 12 lakh crore (~115 billion dollars) of wealth wiped out; market down ~ 2% . Breadth brutal: 435 advances vs ~2,700 declines (1:6) . Nifty closed below 23,300 and below the 10/20/50/200 DMA — described as the steepest fall this financial year . Mid and small caps fell more than benchmarks — complace Infosys Dixon Technologies

docs.title: 2026-02-23 Summary — Business Breakfast, 23 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
docs.text: Summary — Business Breakfast, 23 February 2026 (TV5 Money) Global backdrop GIFT Nifty indicated a gap up of ~150–180 points Monday (it had shown 300+ over the weekend before cooling off). Friday's cash session had risen ~350 points. US tariff uncertainty returned. The US Supreme Court ruled Friday that Trump cannot use IEEPA (International Emergency Economic Powers Act) to impose his blanket tariffs — a ~10 month delayed ruling. Trump is routing around it : invoking Section 122 from 24 February , raising the minimum tariff from 10% to 15% on all countries (India, China, Russia, etc.). Section 122 is valid only 150 days (ordinance like); beyond that he needs another section ( 232 / 301 / Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act , which could allow up to ~50%) or a Congress vote. Effective tariff rate fell from 16% (pre judgment) to ~13.7% post the 15% move (~2.3% lower). Countries previously at 10% (Australia, UK, Singapore, UAE) and the UK's 0% deal rate now move to 15% — flattening the level playing field for India. India–US interim trade pact meeting deferred (today's headline). India had braced to pay 18% ; whether it becomes 15% or 18% is unresolved (final deal possibly April–May). docs.text: Summary — Business Breakfast, 5 January 2026 (TV5 Money) Note: Today's show was hosted from Vizag by Vasanth (anchor/analyst), with Ramakrishna (technical analyst) and, after the break, Kranthi (fundamental analyst) handling viewer Q&A. Kutumba Rao was not present in this episode , so there are no Kutumba Rao stock calls (buys.json is empty). Global backdrop Venezuela dominated the open: the US attacked, removed the country's president (and his wife) to America. Vasanth frames it as a move for control of resources, not "narco terrorism." Venezuela holds the world's largest crude reserves (~300 billion barrels) ; Saudi second at 267 billion , Iran 209 billion . It also holds 161 metric tonnes of gold (~$22.55 billion) , the largest in Latin America. Venezuelan output has collapsed from 3 million bpd to under 1 million bpd ; China is the near sole buyer, so the direct crude price impact is small. Net read for India: positive — "drill, baby, drill" in Venezuela points to more supply, easing oil price risk and reducing the need for Russian oil.
